description: ARGUS Caution List — software that should NOT receive reputation discounts
note: These entries get NO reputation discount. Some may trigger additional YARA scrutiny.

entries.name: AnyDesk
entries.reason: Heavily impersonated by malware. 1,300+ fake AnyDesk sites documented. Legitimate tool but frequently abused for unauthorized remote access.

entries.name: TeamViewer
entries.reason: Major impersonation target. Legitimate remote access tool frequently bundled with scam campaigns and tech support fraud.

entries.name: CCleaner
entries.reason: Suffered a major supply chain attack in 2017 (Piriform/Avast compromise). Current ownership by Gen Digital raises ongoing concerns.

entries.name: uTorrent / BitTorrent
entries.reason: Both products aggressively bundle adware, toolbars, and PUPs in their installers. uTorrent once bundled a cryptocurrency miner.

entries.name: WinZip
entries.reason: Bundles PUPs and adware in free/trial versions. Corel ownership has added aggressive upselling.

entries.name: IObit products (Advanced SystemCare, Driver Booster, Smart Defrag)
entries.reason: Aggressive bundling, misleading 'scan results' designed to pressure purchases, and history of dubious system optimization claims.

entries.name: Avast / AVG
entries.reason: Jumpshot data-selling scandal (2020) — sold detailed browsing data of millions of users. Acquired by Gen Digital (formerly NortonLifeLock).

entries.name: Opera / Opera GX
entries.reason: Acquired by a Chinese consortium in 2016. Opera GX has aggressive data collection. Their predatory loan apps were exposed in 2020.

entries.name: GOM Player / GOM Lab products
entries.reason: History of bundling adware. Korean origin with opaque data practices. Used as a malware distribution vector in targeted attacks.

entries.name: KMPlayer
entries.reason: Bundled with adware and PUPs. Pandora TV ownership with aggressive monetization.

entries.name: Foxit PDF Editor
entries.reason: Free version bundles unwanted software. Foxit Reader has had security vulnerabilities exploited in the wild.

entries.name: DAEMON Tools
entries.reason: Active supply chain attack documented May 2026. Installer compromised to deliver malware. Critical risk.

entries.name: CPU-Z / HWMonitor (CPUID)
entries.reason: Heavily impersonated via fake download sites and Google Ads poisoning (April 2026). Legitimate but a prime impersonation target.

entries.name: Free Download Manager
entries.reason: Suffered a supply chain compromise — malicious Linux package distributed through official site for years before discovery.

entries.name: Cobalt Strike
entries.reason: Legitimate penetration testing tool, but cracked copies are the #1 post-exploitation framework used by ransomware gangs and APT groups.

entries.name: Mimikatz
entries.reason: Credential extraction tool. While it has legitimate security research uses, it is found in virtually every major breach.

entries.name: Generic 'AI Tool' installers
entries.reason: Post-ChatGPT boom produced thousands of fake AI tool installers distributing stealers and RATs. Any AI-branded installer should receive full scrutiny.

entries.name: Generic 'Driver Updater' tools
entries.reason: Almost universally bundled with PUPs, fake scan results, and aggressive upselling. Legitimate driver updates come from hardware vendors.

entries.name: Generic 'VPN' installers
entries.reason: Many free VPN services are data harvesting operations. Kape Technologies (CyberGhost, PIA, ZenMate) has a history as a former adware company.

entries.name: Unsigned Chromium builds
entries.reason: Custom Chromium builds are frequently used as malware delivery vehicles (credential stealers, crypto miners). Only official browser distributions should be trusted.

entries.name: Comodo products
entries.reason: History of issuing fraudulent SSL certificates (2015 incident). Comodo Internet Security has been used as a vector for PUP installation.

entries.name: Qihoo 360 / Tencent security products
entries.reason: Chinese government-linked companies with mandatory data sharing requirements under Chinese law. Opaque data practices for international users.

entries.name: Game cheats / trainers / mod tools
entries.reason: The #1 malware distribution vector targeting gamers. Fake game cheats for Roblox, Minecraft, GTA V, etc. are overwhelmingly malware.

entries.name: Crypto mining software (NiceHash, PhoenixMiner, etc.)
entries.reason: Legitimate when user-installed, but frequently deployed without consent. Presence should always be flagged for user verification.
